title: neottia taizanensis
intro: Neottia taizanensis, also known as Taizan's orchid or Japanese tooth-ache grass, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the orchid family. It is native to Japan and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including toothaches, headaches, and digestive issues, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Neottia taizanensis is typically prepared for medicinal use by boiling its roots and stems in water, then consuming the resulting tea or using it topically as a poultice.
